blocks.content: She smelled like something warm — her shampoo and the night air from outside.
They didn't talk. The floor was full enough that they were pressed a little closer than they'd planned.
He felt her shift — just slightly, leaning in rather than away — and his hand at her waist tightened without him meaning it to.
The song moved. The lights stayed low. Across the room Eli had both hands up, doing something that could generously be called dancing. Kit almost laughed. He looked back down at Daisy.
She was already looking at him.
Just — looking at him. And the noise and the music and the orange ribbon on the basketball hoops all went a little distant, and Kit thought: oh.
Oh, this is what it feels like when you stop pretending.
